metadata.title: YAMA AI — Example Legal Dataset
metadata.description: 100+ Indian legal provisions covering major central acts for seeding and testing
metadata.source: Manually curated from official Indian legislation texts

laws.title: Murder
laws.description: Whoever causes death by doing an act with the intention of causing death, or with the intention of causing such bodily injury as is likely to cause death, or with the knowledge that he is likely by such act to cause death, commits the offence of murder.
laws.keywords: murder, homicide, death, killing, intention to kill
laws.punishment: Death or imprisonment for life, and shall also be liable to fine.

laws.title: Culpable Homicide not amounting to murder
laws.description: Whoever causes death by doing an act with the intention of causing death, or with the intention of causing such bodily injury as is likely to cause death, or with the knowledge that he is likely by such act to cause death, but without premeditation in a sudden fight in the heat of passion upon a sudden quarrel.
laws.keywords: culpable homicide, manslaughter, death, sudden fight, heat of passion
laws.punishment: Imprisonment for life, or imprisonment up to 10 years, and fine.

laws.title: Punishment for murder
laws.description: Whoever commits murder shall be punished with death, or imprisonment for life, and shall also be liable to fine. The court shall record special reasons for not imposing death sentence in cases involving extreme brutality.
laws.keywords: murder punishment, death penalty, life imprisonment, capital punishment
laws.punishment: Death or imprisonment for life, and fine.

laws.title: Dowry Death
laws.description: Where the death of a woman is caused by any burns or bodily injury or occurs otherwise than under normal circumstances within seven years of her marriage and it is shown that soon before her death she was subjected to cruelty or harassment by her husband or any relative of her husband for, or in connection with, any demand for dowry, such death shall be called dowry death.
laws.keywords: dowry death, dowry harassment, bride burning, marital cruelty, seven years
laws.punishment: Imprisonment not less than seven years but which may extend to imprisonment for life.

laws.title: Attempt to murder
laws.description: Whoever does any act with such intention or knowledge and under such circumstances that, if he by that act caused death, he would be guilty of murder, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to ten years, and shall also be liable to fine.
laws.keywords: attempt to murder, attempted homicide, intent to kill, assault
laws.punishment: Imprisonment up to 10 years and fine. If hurt is caused, up to imprisonment for life.

laws.title: Voluntarily causing hurt
laws.description: Whoever does any act with the intention of thereby causing hurt to any person, or with the knowledge that he is likely thereby to cause hurt to any person, and does thereby cause hurt to any person, is said to voluntarily cause hurt.
laws.keywords: hurt, assault, bodily harm, injury, violence
laws.punishment: Imprisonment up to one year, or fine up to ten thousand rupees, or both.

laws.title: Voluntarily causing grievous hurt
laws.description: Whoever voluntarily causes hurt, if the hurt which he intends to cause or knows himself to be likely to cause is grievous hurt, and if the hurt which he causes is grievous hurt, is said voluntarily to cause grievous hurt. Grievous hurt includes emasculation, permanent privation of sight, hearing, or any member or joint, fracture or dislocation of bone.
laws.keywords: grievous hurt, serious injury, fracture, permanent damage, bodily harm
laws.punishment: Imprisonment up to seven years, and fine.

laws.title: Rape
laws.description: A man is said to commit rape if he penetrates or performs sexual acts with a woman under circumstances falling under any of the following descriptions: against her will, without her consent, with consent obtained by putting her in fear of death or hurt, with consent obtained by impersonation of husband, when she is unable to communicate consent, or when she is under eighteen years of age.
laws.keywords: rape, sexual assault, consent, sexual violence, women safety
laws.punishment: Rigorous imprisonment not less than ten years, extendable to imprisonment for life, and fine.

laws.title: Gang Rape
laws.description: Where a woman is raped by one or more persons constituting a group or acting in furtherance of a common intention, each of those persons shall be deemed to have committed the offence of rape and shall be punished.
laws.keywords: gang rape, group sexual assault, common intention, sexual violence
laws.punishment: Rigorous imprisonment not less than twenty years, extendable to life, and fine payable to victim.

laws.title: Assault or use of criminal force to woman with intent to outrage her modesty
laws.description: Whoever assaults or uses criminal force to any woman, intending to outrage or knowing it to be likely that he will thereby outrage her modesty, shall be punished.
laws.keywords: outraging modesty, assault on woman, criminal force, molestation, eve teasing
laws.punishment: Imprisonment not less than one year extendable to five years, and fine.

laws.title: Sexual Harassment
laws.description: A man committing any of the following acts: physical contact and advances involving unwelcome and explicit sexual overtures, a demand or request for sexual favours, showing pornography against the will of a woman, making sexually coloured remarks, shall be guilty of sexual harassment.
laws.keywords: sexual harassment, workplace harassment, unwelcome advances, sexual remarks
laws.punishment: Rigorous imprisonment up to three years, or fine, or both.

laws.title: Voyeurism
laws.description: Any man who watches or captures the image of a woman engaging in a private act in circumstances where she would usually have the expectation of not being observed either by the perpetrator or any other person.
laws.keywords: voyeurism, privacy, watching, recording, private act
laws.punishment: First offence: imprisonment one to three years, and fine. Second conviction: three to seven years, and fine.

laws.title: Stalking
laws.description: Any man who follows a woman and contacts or attempts to contact such woman to foster personal interaction repeatedly despite a clear indication of disinterest by such woman, or monitors the use by a woman of the internet, email or any other form of electronic communication.
laws.keywords: stalking, following, harassment, cyber stalking, persistent contact
laws.punishment: First offence: up to three years and fine. Second offence: up to five years and fine.

laws.title: Cruelty by husband or relatives of husband
laws.description: Whoever, being the husband or the relative of the husband of a woman, subjects such woman to cruelty shall be punished. Cruelty means any wilful conduct which is of such a nature as is likely to drive the woman to commit suicide or to cause grave injury or danger to life, limb or health; or harassment with a view to coercing her to meet any unlawful demand for any property or valuable security.
laws.keywords: domestic violence, cruelty, dowry harassment, husband, marital cruelty
laws.punishment: Imprisonment up to three years and fine.

laws.title: Theft
laws.description: Whoever, intending to take dishonestly any moveable property out of the possession of any person without that person's consent, moves that property in order to such taking, is said to commit theft.
laws.keywords: theft, stealing, dishonest taking, moveable property, stolen
laws.punishment: Imprisonment up to three years, or fine, or both.

laws.title: Robbery
laws.description: In all robbery there is either theft or extortion. When theft is robbery: theft is robbery if, in order to the committing of the theft, or in committing the theft, or in carrying away or attempting to carry away property obtained by the theft, the offender voluntarily causes or attempts to cause death or hurt or wrongful restraint, or fear of instant death, hurt, or wrongful restraint.
laws.keywords: robbery, theft with violence, snatching, armed robbery
laws.punishment: Rigorous imprisonment up to ten years, and fine. If on highway, up to fourteen years.

laws.title: Dacoity
laws.description: When five or more persons conjointly commit or attempt to commit a robbery, or where the whole number of persons conjointly committing or attempting to commit a robbery, and persons present and aiding such commission or attempt, amount to five or more, every person so committing, attempting, or aiding, is said to commit dacoity.
laws.keywords: dacoity, gang robbery, armed gang, five or more persons
laws.punishment: Imprisonment for life, or rigorous imprisonment up to ten years, and fine.

laws.title: Extortion
laws.description: Whoever intentionally puts any person in fear of any injury to that person, or to any other, and thereby dishonestly induces the person so put in fear to deliver to any person any property, or valuable security, or anything signed or sealed which may be converted into a valuable security, commits extortion.
laws.keywords: extortion, blackmail, threat, fear, coercion, demand
laws.punishment: Imprisonment up to three years, or fine, or both.

laws.title: Cheating
laws.description: Whoever, by deceiving any person, fraudulently or dishonestly induces the person so deceived to deliver any property to any person, or to consent that any person shall retain any property, or intentionally induces the person so deceived to do or omit to do anything which he would not do or omit if he were not so deceived.
laws.keywords: cheating, fraud, deception, dishonesty, inducement
laws.punishment: Imprisonment up to three years, or fine, or both.

laws.title: Cheating and dishonestly inducing delivery of property
laws.description: Whoever cheats and thereby dishonestly induces the person deceived to deliver any property to any person, or to make, alter or destroy the whole or any part of a valuable security, or anything which is signed or sealed and is capable of being converted into a valuable security.
laws.keywords: cheating with delivery, property fraud, dishonest inducement
laws.punishment: Imprisonment up to seven years, and fine.

laws.title: Criminal Breach of Trust
laws.description: Whoever, being in any manner entrusted with property, or with any dominion over property, dishonestly misappropriates or converts to his own use that property, or dishonestly uses or disposes of that property in violation of any direction of law prescribing the mode in which such trust is to be discharged.
laws.keywords: breach of trust, misappropriation, dishonesty, fiduciary duty, embezzlement
laws.punishment: Imprisonment up to five years, or fine, or both.

laws.title: Forgery
laws.description: Whoever makes any false document or false electronic record or part of a document or electronic record, with intent to cause damage or injury to the public or to any person, or to support any claim or title, or to cause any person to part with property, or to enter into any express or implied contract, commits forgery.
laws.keywords: forgery, false document, fake, counterfeiting, electronic record
laws.punishment: Imprisonment up to two years, or fine, or both.

laws.title: Criminal Intimidation
laws.description: Whoever threatens another with any injury to his person, reputation or property, or to the person or reputation of any one in whom that person is interested, with intent to cause alarm or to cause that person to do any act which he is not legally bound to do, commits criminal intimidation.
laws.keywords: intimidation, threat, alarm, criminal threat, menace
laws.punishment: Imprisonment up to two years, or fine, or both.

laws.title: Intentional Insult with intent to provoke breach of peace
laws.description: Whoever intentionally insults, and thereby gives provocation to any person, intending or knowing it to be likely that such provocation will cause him to break the public peace, or to commit any other offence.
laws.keywords: insult, provocation, public peace, breach of peace, verbal abuse
laws.punishment: Imprisonment up to two years, or fine, or both.

laws.title: Defamation
laws.description: Whoever, by words either spoken or intended to be read, or by signs or by visible representations, makes or publishes any imputation concerning any person intending to harm, or knowing or having reason to believe that such imputation will harm, the reputation of such person, is said to defame that person.
laws.keywords: defamation, reputation, libel, slander, imputation, character
laws.punishment: Simple imprisonment up to two years, or fine, or both.

laws.title: Act endangering life or personal safety of others
laws.description: Whoever does any act so rashly or negligently as to endanger human life or the personal safety of others, shall be punished. Includes rash driving, negligent acts causing danger to public.
laws.keywords: rash act, negligence, endangering life, personal safety, rash driving
laws.punishment: Imprisonment up to three months, or fine up to two thousand five hundred rupees, or both.

laws.title: Rash driving or riding on a public way
laws.description: Whoever drives any vehicle, or rides, on any public way in a manner so rash or negligent as to endanger human life, or to be likely to cause hurt or injury to any other person.
laws.keywords: rash driving, negligent driving, road accident, traffic offence, public way
laws.punishment: Imprisonment up to six months, or fine up to one thousand rupees, or both.

laws.title: Causing death by negligence
laws.description: Whoever causes the death of any person by doing any rash or negligent act not amounting to culpable homicide.
laws.keywords: death by negligence, accidental death, rash act, negligence, manslaughter
laws.punishment: Imprisonment up to five years, and fine. If by rash or negligent act: up to imprisonment for life.

laws.title: Public nuisance
laws.description: A person is guilty of a public nuisance who does any act or is guilty of an illegal omission which causes any common injury, danger or annoyance to the public or to the people in general who dwell or occupy property in the vicinity.
laws.keywords: public nuisance, common injury, annoyance, neighbourhood, pollution, noise
laws.punishment: Fine up to one thousand rupees.

laws.title: Right of Private Defence
laws.description: Every person has a right, subject to the restrictions contained in this section, to defend his own body and the body of any other person, against any offence affecting the human body. The right of private defence extends to causing death if there is reasonable apprehension of death or grievous hurt.
laws.keywords: self defence, private defence, right to defend, reasonable force, bodily harm
laws.punishment: Not applicable — this section provides a defence/exception.

laws.title: FIR - Information in cognizable cases
laws.description: Every information relating to the commission of a cognizable offence, if given orally to an officer in charge of a police station, shall be reduced to writing by him or under his direction, and be read over to the informant; and every such information, whether given in writing or reduced to writing, shall be signed by the person giving it.
laws.keywords: FIR, first information report, police complaint, cognizable offence, police station

laws.title: Police officer's power to investigate cognizable case
laws.description: Any officer in charge of a police station may, without the order of a Magistrate, investigate any cognizable case which a court having jurisdiction over the local area within the limits of such station would have power to inquire into or try.
laws.keywords: investigation, police power, cognizable case, inquiry, police station

laws.title: Procedure when investigation cannot be completed in twenty-four hours
laws.description: Whenever any person is arrested and detained in custody, and it appears that the investigation cannot be completed within the period of twenty-four hours, the officer in charge of the police station shall transmit to the nearest Judicial Magistrate a copy of the entries in the diary and shall also forward the accused to such Magistrate.
laws.keywords: arrest, detention, 24 hours, judicial magistrate, police custody, remand

laws.title: Bail in case of bailable offence
laws.description: When any person other than a person accused of a non-bailable offence is arrested or detained without warrant by an officer in charge of a police station, or appears or is brought before a Court, and is prepared at any time while in the custody of such officer or at any stage of the proceeding before such Court to give bail, such person shall be released on bail.
laws.keywords: bail, bailable offence, release, surety, police custody, court

laws.title: Bail in case of non-bailable offence
laws.description: When any person accused of, or suspected of, the commission of any non-bailable offence is arrested or detained without warrant by an officer in charge of a police station or appears or is brought before a Court other than the High Court or Court of Session, he may be released on bail. The court shall consider the nature and gravity of the offence, criminal antecedents of the applicant, and possibility of his fleeing from justice.
laws.keywords: bail, non-bailable offence, anticipatory bail, court discretion, surety

laws.title: Anticipatory Bail
laws.description: Where any person has reason to believe that he may be arrested on accusation of having committed a non-bailable offence, he may apply to the High Court or the Court of Session for a direction that in the event of such arrest, he shall be released on bail.
laws.keywords: anticipatory bail, pre-arrest bail, non-bailable, high court, sessions court

laws.title: Chargesheet - Report of police officer on completion of investigation
laws.description: Every investigation shall be completed without unnecessary delay and the report under this section shall be forwarded to the Magistrate. If there is sufficient evidence, a chargesheet shall be filed. The investigation shall be completed within sixty days from the date of first information report for offences punishable with imprisonment up to three years, and ninety days for other offences.
laws.keywords: chargesheet, investigation report, police report, magistrate, FIR closure

laws.title: Order to pay compensation
laws.description: When a Court imposes a sentence of fine or a sentence of which fine forms a part, the Court may order the whole or any part of the fine recovered to be applied in the payment to any person of compensation for any loss or injury caused by the offence.
laws.keywords: compensation, victim compensation, fine, sentence, court order, loss

laws.title: Inherent powers of High Court
laws.description: Nothing in this Sanhita shall be deemed to limit or affect the inherent powers of the High Court to make such orders as may be necessary to give effect to any order under this Sanhita, or to prevent abuse of the process of any Court or otherwise to secure the ends of justice.
laws.keywords: inherent powers, high court, quashing, justice, abuse of process, Section 482

laws.title: Relevancy of electronic records
laws.description: Any information contained in an electronic record which is printed on a paper, stored, recorded or copied in optical or magnetic media produced by a computer shall be deemed to be a document and shall be admissible in any proceedings without further proof of the original.
laws.keywords: electronic evidence, digital record, computer, admissibility, electronic document

laws.title: Confession to police officer not to be proved
laws.description: No confession made to a police officer shall be proved as against a person accused of any offence. No confession made by any person whilst he is in the custody of a police officer, unless it be made in the immediate presence of a Magistrate, shall be proved against such person.
laws.keywords: confession, police, inadmissible, magistrate, custodial statement

laws.title: Dying declaration
laws.description: A statement, written or verbal, of relevant facts made by a person who is dead or who cannot be found, or who has become incapable of giving evidence, or whose attendance cannot be procured, is itself a relevant fact when the statement was made by a person as to the cause of his death, or as to any of the circumstances of the transaction which resulted in his death.
laws.keywords: dying declaration, death statement, relevant fact, deceased, evidence

laws.title: Burden of proof
laws.description: Whoever desires any Court to give judgment as to any legal right or liability dependent on the existence of facts which he asserts, must prove that those facts exist. When a person is bound to prove the existence of any fact, it is said that the burden of proof lies on that person.
laws.keywords: burden of proof, evidence, legal right, assertion, court, liability

laws.title: Presumption of innocence
laws.description: The prosecution must prove the guilt of the accused beyond reasonable doubt. The accused is presumed to be innocent until proven guilty. The burden of proving the guilt of the accused is on the prosecution.
laws.keywords: presumption of innocence, beyond reasonable doubt, prosecution burden, accused rights

laws.title: Equality before law
laws.description: The State shall not deny to any person equality before the law or the equal protection of the laws within the territory of India. This article embodies the concept of rule of law and prohibits class legislation, while permitting reasonable classification.
laws.keywords: equality, equal protection, rule of law, discrimination, classification

laws.title: Protection of certain rights regarding freedom of speech
laws.description: All citizens shall have the right to freedom of speech and expression; to assemble peaceably and without arms; to form associations or unions; to move freely throughout the territory of India; to reside and settle in any part of the territory of India; and to practise any profession, or to carry on any occupation, trade or business.
laws.keywords: freedom of speech, expression, assembly, association, movement, profession, fundamental rights

laws.title: Protection of life and personal liberty
laws.description: No person shall be deprived of his life or personal liberty except according to procedure established by law. This right has been expanded by the Supreme Court to include the right to live with dignity, right to privacy, right to clean environment, right to shelter, right to livelihood, and many other rights.
laws.keywords: right to life, personal liberty, dignity, privacy, procedure established by law

laws.title: Right to Education
laws.description: The State shall provide free and compulsory education to all children of the age of six to fourteen years in such manner as the State may, by law, determine. Added by the 86th Amendment Act, 2002.
laws.keywords: right to education, free education, compulsory education, children, fundamental right

laws.title: Protection against arrest and detention in certain cases
laws.description: No person who is arrested shall be detained in custody without being informed of the grounds for such arrest nor shall he be denied the right to consult and be defended by a legal practitioner of his choice. Every person arrested shall be produced before the nearest magistrate within twenty-four hours.
laws.keywords: arrest protection, detention, grounds of arrest, legal practitioner, 24 hours, magistrate

laws.title: Remedies for enforcement of fundamental rights
laws.description: The right to move the Supreme Court by appropriate proceedings for the enforcement of fundamental rights is guaranteed. The Supreme Court shall have power to issue directions or orders or writs, including writs in the nature of habeas corpus, mandamus, prohibition, quo warranto and certiorari.
laws.keywords: writ petition, supreme court, habeas corpus, mandamus, fundamental rights enforcement

laws.title: Power of High Courts to issue writs
laws.description: Every High Court shall have power to issue to any person or authority, including the Government, directions, orders or writs, including writs in the nature of habeas corpus, mandamus, prohibition, quo warranto and certiorari, for the enforcement of any of the fundamental rights and for any other purpose.
laws.keywords: high court writ, writ jurisdiction, habeas corpus, mandamus, fundamental rights, any other purpose

laws.title: Prohibition of discrimination on grounds of religion, race, caste, sex or place of birth
laws.description: The State shall not discriminate against any citizen on grounds only of religion, race, caste, sex, place of birth or any of them. No citizen shall be subjected to any disability, liability, restriction or condition with regard to access to shops, public restaurants, hotels and places of public entertainment, or the use of wells, tanks, bathing ghats, roads and places of public resort maintained by the State.
laws.keywords: discrimination, religion, race, caste, sex, place of birth, equality, reservation

laws.title: Protection in respect of conviction for offences
laws.description: No person shall be convicted of any offence except for violation of the law in force at the time of the commission of the act charged as an offence, nor be subjected to a penalty greater than that which might have been inflicted under the law in force at the time. No person shall be prosecuted and punished for the same offence more than once. No person accused of any offence shall be compelled to be a witness against himself.
laws.keywords: ex post facto law, double jeopardy, self-incrimination, conviction protection, retrospective law

laws.title: Freedom of conscience and free profession, practice and propagation of religion
laws.description: Subject to public order, morality and health, all persons are equally entitled to freedom of conscience and the right freely to profess, practise and propagate religion.
laws.keywords: freedom of religion, conscience, propagation, religious freedom, secular

laws.title: Penalty for damage to computer system
laws.description: If any person without permission of the owner or any other person in charge of a computer, computer system or computer network, accesses or secures access, downloads, copies or extracts any data or information, introduces or causes to be introduced any computer contaminant or computer virus, damages or causes to be damaged any computer, denies or causes denial of access, he shall be liable to pay damages by way of compensation to the person so affected.
laws.keywords: hacking, unauthorized access, computer damage, virus, data theft, cyber crime
laws.punishment: Compensation up to five crore rupees to affected person.

laws.title: Computer related offences
laws.description: If any person, dishonestly or fraudulently, does any act referred to in section 43, he shall be punishable with imprisonment for a term which may extend to three years or with fine which may extend to five lakh rupees or with both.
laws.keywords: cyber crime, computer offence, hacking, dishonest, fraudulent, digital crime
laws.punishment: Imprisonment up to three years, or fine up to five lakh rupees, or both.

laws.title: Punishment for sending offensive messages (Struck down)
laws.description: This section was struck down by the Supreme Court of India in Shreya Singhal v. Union of India (2015) as unconstitutional for violating Article 19(1)(a) of the Constitution. The section had penalized sending offensive or menacing messages through communication services.
laws.keywords: offensive messages, struck down, unconstitutional, Shreya Singhal, free speech, online speech
laws.punishment: STRUCK DOWN by Supreme Court in 2015. No longer applicable.

laws.title: Punishment for identity theft
laws.description: Whoever, fraudulently or dishonestly make use of the electronic signature, password or any other unique identification feature of any other person, shall be punished.
laws.keywords: identity theft, electronic signature, password theft, impersonation, fraud
laws.punishment: Imprisonment up to three years and fine up to one lakh rupees.

laws.title: Punishment for cheating by personation using computer resource
laws.description: Whoever, by means of any communication device or computer resource cheats by personation, shall be punished with imprisonment and fine.
laws.keywords: online cheating, impersonation, phishing, computer fraud, personation
laws.punishment: Imprisonment up to three years and fine up to one lakh rupees.

laws.title: Punishment for violation of privacy
laws.description: Whoever, intentionally or knowingly captures, publishes or transmits the image of a private area of any person without his or her consent, under circumstances violating the privacy of that person, shall be punished.
laws.keywords: privacy violation, intimate images, non-consensual, voyeurism, digital privacy
laws.punishment: Imprisonment up to three years, or fine not exceeding two lakh rupees, or both.

laws.title: Punishment for publishing or transmitting obscene material in electronic form
laws.description: Whoever publishes or transmits or causes to be published or transmitted in the electronic form, any material which is lascivious or appeals to the prurient interest or if its effect is such as to tend to deprave and corrupt persons who are likely to read the matter.
laws.keywords: obscene content, pornography, electronic form, online obscenity, publish
laws.punishment: First conviction: imprisonment up to three years and fine up to five lakh. Subsequent: five years and ten lakh.

laws.title: Punishment for publishing sexually explicit material in electronic form
laws.description: Whoever publishes or transmits or causes to be published or transmitted in the electronic form any material which contains sexually explicit act or conduct.
laws.keywords: sexually explicit, electronic material, pornography, sexual content, online
laws.punishment: First conviction: imprisonment up to five years and fine up to ten lakh. Subsequent: seven years and ten lakh.

laws.title: Punishment for publishing child sexually explicit material in electronic form
laws.description: Whoever publishes or transmits or causes to be published or transmitted material in electronic form which depicts children engaged in sexually explicit act or conduct, or creates text or digital images, collects, seeks, browses, downloads, advertises, promotes, exchanges or distributes material depicting children in obscene or indecent manner.
laws.keywords: child pornography, CSAM, child exploitation, electronic material, minors
laws.punishment: First conviction: imprisonment up to five years and fine up to ten lakh. Subsequent: seven years and ten lakh.

laws.title: Breach of confidentiality and privacy
laws.description: Any person who, in pursuance of any of the powers conferred under this Act, rules or regulations made thereunder, has secured access to any electronic record, book, register, correspondence, information, document or other material without the consent of the person concerned discloses such information to any other person.
laws.keywords: breach of confidentiality, privacy, disclosure, unauthorized, electronic record
laws.punishment: Imprisonment up to two years, or fine up to one lakh rupees, or both.

laws.title: Definition of Consumer
laws.description: Consumer means any person who buys any goods for a consideration which has been paid or promised or partly paid and partly promised, or under any system of deferred payment, and includes any user of such goods other than the person who buys such goods for resale or for any commercial purpose. It also includes a person who hires or avails of any service for a consideration.
laws.keywords: consumer definition, buyer, goods, services, consideration, commercial purpose

laws.title: District Consumer Disputes Redressal Commission
laws.description: A complaint relating to goods or services where the value does not exceed one crore rupees shall be filed before the District Commission. The District Commission shall have the power to adjudicate complaints and provide remedies including replacement of goods, refund, compensation for losses, and punitive damages.
laws.keywords: consumer complaint, district commission, consumer forum, redressal, compensation

laws.title: Complaints to District Commission
laws.description: A complaint may be filed by the consumer to whom the goods are sold or delivered or agreed to be sold or delivered, or the service provided or agreed to be provided; any recognised consumer association; the Central Government or State Government; the Central Authority; or one or more consumers, where there are numerous consumers having the same interest.
laws.keywords: consumer complaint, filing, district commission, who can file, class action

laws.title: State Consumer Disputes Redressal Commission
laws.description: The State Commission shall have jurisdiction to entertain complaints where the value of the goods or services exceeds one crore rupees but does not exceed ten crore rupees. It also handles appeals against orders of District Commission.
laws.keywords: state commission, consumer dispute, appeal, jurisdiction, one crore, ten crore

laws.title: Product Liability
laws.description: A product liability action may be brought by a complainant against a product manufacturer or a product service provider or a product seller for any harm caused to the consumer by a defective product manufactured by the product manufacturer. Harm includes damage to property, personal injury, illness or death.
laws.keywords: product liability, defective product, manufacturer liability, harm, compensation, injury

laws.title: Definition of Misleading Advertisement
laws.description: Misleading advertisement means any advertisement which falsely describes any goods or services, gives a false guarantee, or is likely to mislead the consumers as to the nature, substance, quantity, quality of goods or services, or conveys an express or implied representation which constitutes an unfair trade practice.
laws.keywords: misleading advertisement, false advertising, unfair trade practice, consumer deception

laws.title: Necessity for driving licence
laws.description: No person shall drive a motor vehicle in any public place unless he holds an effective driving licence issued to him authorizing him to drive the vehicle. The driving licence must be valid and appropriate for the class of vehicle being driven.
laws.keywords: driving licence, license requirement, public place, motor vehicle, authorization
laws.punishment: Fine up to five thousand rupees or imprisonment up to three months or both.

laws.title: Limits of speed
laws.description: No person shall drive a motor vehicle in any public place at a speed exceeding the maximum speed or below the minimum speed fixed for the area or the class of the vehicle by the State Government.
laws.keywords: speed limit, overspeeding, rash driving, traffic violation, motor vehicle speed
laws.punishment: Fine for first offence up to two thousand rupees. Second offence up to four thousand rupees.

laws.title: Driving dangerously
laws.description: Whoever drives a motor vehicle at a speed or in a manner which is dangerous to the public, having regard to all the circumstances of the case including the nature, condition and use of the place, and the amount of traffic which at the time is or might reasonably be expected to be in the place, shall be punishable.
laws.keywords: dangerous driving, rash driving, reckless driving, traffic danger, road safety
laws.punishment: First offence: imprisonment up to one year or fine up to five thousand or both. Subsequent: up to two years or ten thousand or both.

laws.title: Driving by a drunken person or under the influence of drugs
laws.description: Whoever, while driving or attempting to drive a motor vehicle, has in his blood alcohol exceeding 30 mg per 100 ml of blood detected in a test by a breath analyser, or is under the influence of a drug to such an extent as to be incapable of exercising proper control over the vehicle, shall be punishable.
laws.keywords: drunk driving, alcohol, DUI, drugs, breath analyser, blood alcohol, intoxication
laws.punishment: First offence: imprisonment up to six months or fine up to ten thousand or both. Subsequent: up to two years or fifteen thousand or both.

laws.title: Application for compensation
laws.description: An application for compensation arising out of an accident of the nature specified in sub-section (1) of section 165 may be made by the person who has sustained the injury, or by the owner of the property, or where death has resulted from the accident, by all or any of the legal representatives of the deceased, or by any agent duly authorised by the person injured or the legal representatives of the deceased.
laws.keywords: motor accident compensation, claim, injury, death, legal representatives, tribunal

laws.title: Liability to pay compensation in certain cases on the principle of no fault
laws.description: Where death or permanent disablement of any person has resulted from an accident arising out of the use of a motor vehicle, the owner of the vehicle shall be liable to pay compensation in respect of such death or permanent disablement, irrespective of fault.
laws.keywords: no fault liability, motor accident, compensation, death, permanent disablement, strict liability

laws.act_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005
laws.title: Definition of domestic violence
laws.description: Any act, omission or commission or conduct of the respondent shall constitute domestic violence in case it harms or injures or endangers the health, safety, life, limb or well-being, whether mental or physical, of the aggrieved person or tends to do so and includes causing physical abuse, sexual abuse, verbal and emotional abuse and economic abuse.
laws.keywords: domestic violence, physical abuse, sexual abuse, emotional abuse, economic abuse, women protection

laws.act_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005
laws.title: Application to Magistrate
laws.description: An aggrieved person or a Protection Officer or any other person on behalf of the aggrieved person may present an application to the Magistrate seeking relief including protection orders, residence orders, monetary relief, custody orders, and compensation orders.
laws.keywords: domestic violence complaint, magistrate, protection order, residence order, monetary relief

laws.act_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005
laws.title: Protection Orders
laws.description: The Magistrate may, after giving the aggrieved person and the respondent an opportunity of being heard, pass a protection order in favour of the aggrieved person prohibiting the respondent from committing any act of domestic violence, aiding or abetting domestic violence, entering the workplace or any other place frequented by the aggrieved person, attempting to communicate in any form with the aggrieved person.
laws.keywords: protection order, restraining order, domestic violence, prohibition, magistrate, no contact

laws.act_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005
laws.title: Residence Orders
laws.description: The Magistrate may, on being satisfied that domestic violence has taken place, pass a residence order restraining the respondent from dispossessing or disturbing the possession of the aggrieved person from the shared household, or directing the respondent to remove himself from the shared household.
laws.keywords: residence order, shared household, dispossession, domestic violence, right to reside

laws.act_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005
laws.title: Monetary Relief
laws.description: The aggrieved person may seek monetary relief to meet the expenses incurred and losses suffered by the aggrieved person and any child of the aggrieved person as a result of domestic violence including loss of earnings, medical expenses, loss caused due to destruction or damage of property, and maintenance for the aggrieved person and her children.
laws.keywords: monetary relief, maintenance, medical expenses, domestic violence compensation, financial support

laws.title: Right to information
laws.description: Subject to the provisions of this Act, all citizens shall have the right to information. The right to information includes the right to inspection of work, documents, records; taking notes, extracts, or certified copies of documents or records; and taking certified samples of material.
laws.keywords: right to information, RTI, transparency, citizen right, inspection, documents

laws.title: Request for obtaining information
laws.description: A person who desires to obtain any information under this Act shall make a request in writing or through electronic means in English or Hindi or in the official language of the area in which the application is being made, accompanied by the prescribed fee. The applicant is not required to give any reason for requesting the information.
laws.keywords: RTI application, information request, fee, no reason required, public authority

laws.title: Disposal of request
laws.description: The Central Public Information Officer or State Public Information Officer shall, as expeditiously as possible, and in any case within thirty days of the receipt of the request, either provide the information on payment of such fee as may be prescribed or reject the request for reasons specified. In case of information concerning life or liberty, it shall be provided within forty-eight hours.
laws.keywords: RTI response, 30 days, information disposal, rejection, life and liberty, 48 hours

laws.title: Penalties
laws.description: Where the Central Information Commission or the State Information Commission is of the opinion that the Central Public Information Officer or the State Public Information Officer has, without any reasonable cause, refused to receive an application, or has not furnished information within the time specified, or has malafidely denied the request, it shall impose a penalty of two hundred and fifty rupees each day until the application is received or information is furnished, up to maximum of twenty-five thousand rupees.
laws.keywords: RTI penalty, information officer, refusal, delay, fine, twenty-five thousand
laws.punishment: Penalty of Rs. 250 per day of delay, up to Rs. 25,000.

laws.title: Organized Crime
laws.description: Any continuing unlawful activity including kidnapping, robbery, vehicle theft, extortion, land grabbing, contract killing, economic offence, cyber-crimes committed by a member of such group or gang individually or jointly, either as a member of such group or gang or on behalf of such group or gang, and commission of such offence shall be a cognizable and non-bailable offence punishable under this Act.
laws.keywords: organized crime, gang, syndicate, continuing unlawful activity, mafia, criminal group
laws.punishment: If offence results in death: death or imprisonment for life and fine of not less than ten lakh. Otherwise: imprisonment not less than five years extendable to life and fine not less than five lakh.
laws.old_law_reference: No equivalent in IPC. New provision in BNS 2023.

laws.title: Petty Organized Crime
laws.description: Whoever, being a member of a group or gang, either individually or jointly, commits any of the following offences, including theft, snatching, cheating, unauthorized selling of tickets, unauthorized betting or gambling, selling of public examination question papers, shall be guilty of petty organized crime.
laws.keywords: petty crime, snatching, ticket selling, gambling, organized group, minor offence
laws.punishment: Imprisonment from one to seven years, and fine.
laws.old_law_reference: No equivalent in IPC. New provision in BNS 2023.

laws.title: Terrorist Act
laws.description: Whoever does any act with the intent to threaten or likely to threaten the unity, integrity, sovereignty, security, or economic security of India, or with the intent to strike terror or likely to strike terror in the people or any section of the people in India or in any foreign country.
laws.keywords: terrorism, terrorist act, national security, bomb, explosive, threat to unity
laws.punishment: Death or imprisonment for life, and shall also be liable to fine, if such act has resulted in death. Otherwise: imprisonment not less than five years extendable to life, and fine not less than five lakh.
laws.old_law_reference: UAPA provisions. New addition to BNS 2023.

laws.title: Sexual intercourse by employing deceitful means etc.
laws.description: Whoever, by deceitful means or making a promise to marry the woman which he does not intend to fulfil, has sexual intercourse with a woman, which is not a case of rape, shall be punished.
laws.keywords: false promise of marriage, deceit, sexual intercourse, consent by fraud, cheating woman
laws.punishment: Imprisonment up to ten years, and shall also be liable to fine.
laws.old_law_reference: No direct equivalent in IPC. New provision in BNS 2023.

laws.title: Snatching
laws.description: Whoever commits theft, with the intent to cause hurt or fear of hurt to any person, by suddenly or quickly or forcibly seizing or securing or grabbing from any person any moveable property, is said to commit snatching.
laws.keywords: snatching, chain snatching, mobile snatching, theft with force, sudden seizure
laws.punishment: Imprisonment up to three years, and shall also be liable to fine.
laws.old_law_reference: No equivalent in IPC. New provision in BNS 2023.

laws.title: Acts endangering sovereignty, unity and integrity of India
laws.description: Whoever, purposely or knowingly, by words, either spoken or written, or by signs, or by visible representation, or by electronic communication or by use of financial means, or otherwise, excites or attempts to excite, secession or armed rebellion or subversive activities, or encourages feelings of separatist activities or endangers sovereignty or unity and integrity of India.
laws.keywords: sedition replacement, sovereignty, unity, integrity, separatism, anti-national, subversion
laws.punishment: Imprisonment for life, or imprisonment up to seven years, and fine.
laws.old_law_reference: Indian Penal Code, Section 124A (Sedition — now replaced).

laws.title: Promoting enmity between different groups
laws.description: Whoever promotes or attempts to promote, on grounds of religion, race, place of birth, residence, language, caste or community or any other ground whatsoever, disharmony or feelings of enmity, hatred or ill-will between different religious, racial, language or regional groups or castes or communities.
laws.keywords: enmity, hatred, communal, religion, caste, disharmony, hate speech, incitement
laws.punishment: Imprisonment up to three years, or fine, or both.

laws.title: Interpretation clause - Definitions
laws.description: Defines key terms: (a) When one person signifies to another his willingness to do or abstain from doing anything, with a view to obtaining the assent of that other, he is said to make a proposal. (b) When the person to whom the proposal is made signifies his assent thereto, the proposal is said to be accepted. (c) A proposal when accepted becomes a promise. (d) The person making the proposal is called the promisor, and the person accepting is called the promisee. (e) Every promise and every set of promises, forming the consideration for each other, is an agreement. (f) Promises which form the consideration or part thereof, are called reciprocal promises. (g) An agreement not enforceable by law is said to be void. (h) An agreement enforceable by law is a contract. (i) An agreement which is enforceable by law at the option of one or more parties, but not at the option of others, is a voidable contract. (j) A contract which ceases to be enforceable by law becomes void when it ceases to be enforceable.
laws.keywords: proposal, acceptance, promise, agreement, contract, void, voidable, promisor, promisee, consideration
laws.punishment: Civil remedies: Specific performance, damages, injunction

laws.title: What agreements are contracts
laws.description: All agreements are contracts if they are made by the free consent of parties competent to contract, for a lawful consideration and with a lawful object, and are not expressly declared to be void.
laws.keywords: valid contract, free consent, competent parties, lawful consideration, lawful object, essential elements
laws.punishment: Civil remedies: Specific performance, damages

laws.title: Who are competent to contract
laws.description: Every person is competent to contract who is of the age of majority according to the law to which he is subject, and who is of sound mind and is not disqualified from contracting by any law to which he is subject.
laws.keywords: capacity, competency, minor, unsound mind, disqualified, age of majority
laws.punishment: Contract by minor is void ab initio

laws.title: Consent defined
laws.description: Two or more persons are said to consent when they agree upon the same thing in the same sense.
laws.keywords: consent, consensus ad idem, mutual agreement, meeting of minds
laws.punishment: Civil remedies

laws.title: Free consent defined
laws.description: Consent is said to be free when it is not caused by (1) coercion, as defined in section 15, or (2) undue influence, as defined in section 16, or (3) fraud, as defined in section 17, or (4) misrepresentation, as defined in section 18, or (5) mistake, subject to the provisions of sections 20, 21 and 22.
laws.keywords: free consent, coercion, undue influence, fraud, misrepresentation, mistake, voidable
laws.punishment: Contract voidable at the option of aggrieved party

laws.title: Coercion defined
laws.description: Coercion is the committing, or threatening to commit, any act forbidden by the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita (formerly Indian Penal Code), or the unlawful detaining, or threatening to detain, any property, to the prejudice of any person whatever, with the intention of causing any person to enter into an agreement.
laws.keywords: coercion, threat, force, unlawful detention, criminal intimidation, forced agreement
laws.punishment: Contract voidable; may also attract criminal liability under BNS

laws.title: Undue influence defined
laws.description: A contract is said to be induced by undue influence where the relations subsisting between the parties are such that one of the parties is in a position to dominate the will of the other and uses that position to obtain an unfair advantage over the other. A person is deemed to be in a position to dominate the will of another when he holds a real or apparent authority over the other, or where he stands in a fiduciary relation to the other, or where he makes a contract with a person whose mental capacity is temporarily or permanently affected.
laws.keywords: undue influence, domination, fiduciary, authority, unfair advantage, mental capacity
laws.punishment: Contract voidable at option of aggrieved party

laws.title: Fraud defined
laws.description: Fraud means and includes any of the following acts committed by a party to a contract with intent to deceive another party or to induce him to enter into the contract: (1) the suggestion that a fact is true when it is not true by one who does not believe it to be true; (2) the active concealment of a fact by one having knowledge or belief of the fact; (3) a promise made without any intention of performing it; (4) any other act fitted to deceive; (5) any such act or omission as the law specially declares to be fraudulent.
laws.keywords: fraud, deception, false representation, concealment, cheating, false promise
laws.punishment: Contract voidable; damages recoverable; may attract criminal fraud charges

laws.title: Misrepresentation defined
laws.description: Misrepresentation means and includes: (1) the positive assertion, in a manner not warranted by the information of the person making it, of that which is not true, though he believes it to be true; (2) any breach of duty which, without an intent to deceive, gains an advantage to the person committing it by misleading another to his prejudice; (3) causing, however innocently, a party to an agreement, to make a mistake as to the substance of the thing which is the subject of the agreement.
laws.keywords: misrepresentation, false statement, innocent misrepresentation, misleading
laws.punishment: Contract voidable; may claim damages

laws.title: What considerations and objects are lawful
laws.description: The consideration or object of an agreement is lawful, unless it is forbidden by law; or is of such a nature that, if permitted, it would defeat the provisions of any law; or is fraudulent; or involves or implies injury to the person or property of another; or the Court regards it as immoral or opposed to public policy.
laws.keywords: lawful consideration, lawful object, forbidden by law, fraudulent, immoral, public policy
laws.punishment: Agreement is void

laws.title: Agreement without consideration void
laws.description: An agreement made without consideration is void unless: (1) it is expressed in writing and registered under the law, and is made on account of natural love and affection between parties standing in a near relation; (2) it is a promise to compensate a person who has already voluntarily done something for the promisor; (3) it is a promise to pay a debt barred by the limitation law.
laws.keywords: consideration, void agreement, natural love affection, voluntary service, time-barred debt
laws.punishment: Agreement void and unenforceable

laws.title: Agreement in restraint of trade void
laws.description: Every agreement by which any one is restrained from exercising a lawful profession, trade or business of any kind, is to that extent void. Exception: One who sells the goodwill of a business may agree with the buyer to refrain from carrying on a similar business, within specified local limits, so long as the buyer carries on a like business therein.
laws.keywords: restraint of trade, non-compete, void agreement, goodwill, business restriction
laws.punishment: Agreement void to extent of restraint

laws.title: Agreements in restraint of legal proceedings void
laws.description: Every agreement by which any party thereto is restricted absolutely from enforcing his rights under or in respect of any contract, by the usual legal proceedings in the ordinary tribunals, or which limits the time within which he may thus enforce his rights, is void to that extent. Exception: This section shall not affect the validity of contracts referring disputes to arbitration.
laws.keywords: legal proceedings, restriction, arbitration, limitation, ouster of jurisdiction
laws.punishment: Agreement void to that extent

laws.title: Agreement to do impossible act / Frustration
laws.description: An agreement to do an act impossible in itself is void. A contract to do an act which, after the contract is made, becomes impossible, or, by reason of some event which the promisor could not prevent, unlawful, becomes void when the act becomes impossible or unlawful. Where one person has promised to do something which he knew or with reasonable diligence might have known to be impossible or unlawful, such promisor must make compensation.
laws.keywords: impossible act, frustration, void contract, supervening impossibility, force majeure
laws.punishment: Contract becomes void; compensation may be payable

laws.title: Compensation for loss or damage caused by breach
laws.description: When a contract has been broken, the party who suffers by such breach is entitled to receive, from the party who has broken the contract, compensation for any loss or damage caused to him thereby, which naturally arose in the usual course of things from such breach, or which the parties knew, when they made the contract, to be likely to result from the breach of it.
laws.keywords: breach of contract, damages, compensation, loss, consequential damages, Hadley v Baxendale
laws.punishment: Monetary compensation/damages

laws.title: Compensation for breach where penalty stipulated
laws.description: When a contract has been broken, if a sum is named in the contract as the amount to be paid in case of such breach, or if the contract contains any other stipulation by way of penalty, the party complaining of the breach is entitled, whether or not actual damage or loss is proved to have been caused thereby, to receive from the party who has broken the contract reasonable compensation not exceeding the amount so named or the penalty stipulated for.
laws.keywords: penalty clause, liquidated damages, breach compensation, stipulated sum
laws.punishment: Reasonable compensation up to stipulated amount

laws.title: Contract of indemnity defined
laws.description: A contract by which one party promises to save the other from loss caused to him by the conduct of the promisor himself, or by the conduct of any other person, is called a contract of indemnity.
laws.keywords: indemnity, save harmless, loss protection, indemnifier, indemnity holder
laws.punishment: Civil remedies: Claim for indemnification

laws.title: Contract of guarantee defined
laws.description: A contract of guarantee is a contract to perform the promise, or discharge the liability, of a third person in case of his default. The person who gives the guarantee is called the surety; the person in respect of whose default the guarantee is given is called the principal debtor, and the person to whom the guarantee is given is called the creditor.
laws.keywords: guarantee, surety, principal debtor, creditor, default, guarantor, co-signer
laws.punishment: Surety liable on default of principal debtor

laws.title: Bailment, bailor and bailee defined
laws.description: A bailment is the delivery of goods by one person to another for some purpose, upon a contract that they shall, when the purpose is accomplished, be returned or otherwise disposed of according to the directions of the person delivering them. The person delivering the goods is called the bailor. The person to whom they are delivered is called the bailee.
laws.keywords: bailment, bailor, bailee, delivery of goods, custody, return of goods
laws.punishment: Civil remedies for breach of bailment terms

laws.title: Pledge defined
laws.description: The bailment of goods as security for payment of a debt or performance of a promise is called pledge. The bailor is in this case called the pawnor. The bailee is called the pawnee.
laws.keywords: pledge, pawn, security, pawnor, pawnee, collateral, debt security
laws.punishment: Civil remedies; pawnee has right to retain and sell

laws.title: Agent and principal defined
laws.description: An agent is a person employed to do any act for another, or to represent another in dealings with third persons. The person for whom such act is done, or who is so represented, is called the principal.
laws.keywords: agency, agent, principal, representation, authority, power of attorney
laws.punishment: Civil liability based on agency relationship

laws.title: Termination of agency
laws.description: An agency is terminated by the principal revoking his authority; or by the agent renouncing the business of the agency; or by the business of the agency being completed; or by either the principal or agent dying or becoming of unsound mind; or by the principal being adjudicated an insolvent under the provisions of any Act for the time being in force for the relief of insolvent debtors.
laws.keywords: termination of agency, revocation, renunciation, death, insolvency, agency end
laws.punishment: Civil consequences of termination

laws.title: Dishonour of cheque for insufficiency of funds
laws.description: Where any cheque drawn by a person on an account maintained by him with a banker for payment of any amount of money to another person from out of that account for the discharge, in whole or in part, of any debt or other liability, is returned by the bank unpaid, either because of the amount of money standing to the credit of that account is insufficient to honour the cheque or that it exceeds the amount arranged to be paid from that account by an agreement made with that bank, such person shall be deemed to have committed an offence.
laws.keywords: cheque bounce, dishonour, insufficient funds, bounced cheque, payment default, NI Act
laws.punishment: Imprisonment up to two years, or fine up to twice the cheque amount, or both.

laws.title: Presumption in favour of holder
laws.description: It shall be presumed, unless the contrary is proved, that the holder of a cheque received the cheque of the nature referred to in section 138 for the discharge, in whole or in part, of any debt or other liability.
laws.keywords: presumption, cheque holder, debt discharge, legally enforceable debt
laws.punishment: N/A - Procedural provision

laws.title: Offences by companies
laws.description: If the person committing an offence under section 138 is a company, every person who, at the time the offence was committed, was in charge of, and was responsible to, the company for the conduct of the business of the company, as well as the company, shall be deemed to be guilty of the offence.
laws.keywords: company offence, director liability, vicarious liability, corporate liability, cheque bounce company
laws.punishment: Same as Section 138 - imprisonment up to two years or fine or both

laws.title: Cognizance of offences
laws.description: Notwithstanding anything contained in the Code of Criminal Procedure, 1973, (a) no court shall take cognizance of any offence punishable under section 138 except upon a complaint, in writing, made by the payee or the holder in due course of the cheque; (b) such complaint is made within one month of the date on which the cause of action arises under clause (c) of the proviso to section 138.
laws.keywords: cognizance, complaint filing, limitation, one month, payee, holder in due course
laws.punishment: N/A - Procedural provision regarding filing

laws.title: Power of court to try cases summarily
laws.description: Notwithstanding anything contained in the Code of Criminal Procedure, 1973, all offences under this Chapter shall be tried by a Judicial Magistrate of the first class or by a Metropolitan Magistrate and the provisions of sections 262 to 265 of the said Code shall apply to the trial of such offences.
laws.keywords: summary trial, magistrate, jurisdiction, quick trial, cheque case trial
laws.punishment: N/A - Procedural provision

laws.title: Offences to be compoundable
laws.description: Notwithstanding anything contained in the Code of Criminal Procedure, 1973, every offence punishable under this Act shall be compoundable.
laws.keywords: compoundable, settlement, compromise, cheque case settlement
laws.punishment: N/A - Allows settlement/compounding

laws.title: Theft
laws.description: Whoever, intending to take dishonestly any moveable property out of the possession of any person without that persons consent, moves that property in order to such taking, is said to commit theft.
laws.keywords: theft, stealing, moveable property, dishonest intention, taking without consent
laws.punishment: Imprisonment up to three years, or fine, or both.

laws.title: Theft in dwelling house
laws.description: Whoever commits theft in any building, tent or vessel, which building, tent or vessel is used as a human dwelling, or used for the custody of property.
laws.keywords: theft in dwelling, house theft, burglary, building theft, residential theft
laws.punishment: Imprisonment up to seven years, and shall also be liable to fine.

laws.title: Extortion
laws.description: Whoever intentionally puts any person in fear of any injury to that person or to any other, and thereby dishonestly induces the person so put in fear to deliver to any person any property or valuable security, or anything signed or sealed which may be converted into a valuable security, commits extortion.
laws.keywords: extortion, threat, fear, inducing delivery, valuable security, blackmail
laws.punishment: Imprisonment up to three years, or fine, or both.

laws.title: Robbery
laws.description: In all robbery there is either theft or extortion. When theft is robbery: Theft is robbery if, in order to commit the theft, or in committing the theft, or in carrying away or attempting to carry away property obtained by the theft, the offender, for that end, voluntarily causes or attempts to cause death or hurt or wrongful restraint, or fear of instant death or of instant hurt, or of instant wrongful restraint.
laws.keywords: robbery, theft with violence, hurt, fear, wrongful restraint, violent theft
laws.punishment: Rigorous imprisonment up to ten years, and shall also be liable to fine.

laws.title: Dacoity
laws.description: When five or more persons conjointly commit or attempt to commit a robbery, or where the whole number of persons conjointly committing or attempting to commit a robbery, and persons present and aiding such commission or attempt, amount to five or more, every person so committing, attempting or aiding, is said to commit dacoity.
laws.keywords: dacoity, gang robbery, five or more persons, conjoint robbery, armed robbery
laws.punishment: Rigorous imprisonment for a term not less than five years but may extend to imprisonment for life, and shall also be liable to fine.

laws.title: Criminal breach of trust
laws.description: Whoever, being in any manner entrusted with property, or with any dominion over property, dishonestly misappropriates or converts to his own use that property, or dishonestly uses or disposes of that property in violation of any direction of law prescribing the mode in which such trust is to be discharged, or of any legal contract, express or implied, which he has made touching the discharge of such trust, or wilfully suffers any other person so to do, commits criminal breach of trust.
laws.keywords: criminal breach of trust, CBT, misappropriation, entrusted property, fiduciary duty
laws.punishment: Imprisonment up to three years, or fine, or both.

laws.title: Criminal breach of trust by carrier, wharfinger etc.
laws.description: Whoever, being entrusted with property as a carrier, wharfinger or warehouse-keeper, commits criminal breach of trust in respect of such property.
laws.keywords: carrier breach of trust, warehouse keeper, wharfinger, transport, logistics fraud
laws.punishment: Imprisonment up to seven years, and shall also be liable to fine.

laws.title: Criminal breach of trust by clerk or servant
laws.description: Whoever, being a clerk or servant or employed as a clerk or servant, and being in any manner entrusted in such capacity with property, or with any dominion over property, commits criminal breach of trust in respect of that property.
laws.keywords: employee breach of trust, clerk, servant, employment fraud, office theft
laws.punishment: Imprisonment up to seven years, and shall also be liable to fine.

laws.title: Criminal breach of trust by public servant, banker, merchant or agent
laws.description: Whoever, being in any manner entrusted with property, or with any dominion over property in his capacity of a public servant or in the way of his business as a banker, merchant, factor, broker, attorney or agent, commits criminal breach of trust in respect of that property.
laws.keywords: public servant CBT, banker fraud, agent fraud, broker fraud, professional breach of trust
laws.punishment: Imprisonment for life, or imprisonment up to ten years, and shall also be liable to fine.

laws.title: Cheating
laws.description: Whoever, by deceiving any person, fraudulently or dishonestly induces the person so deceived to deliver any property to any person, or to consent that any person shall retain any property, or intentionally induces the person so deceived to do or omit to do anything which he would not do or omit if he were not so deceived, and which act or omission causes or is likely to cause damage or harm to that person in body, mind, reputation or property, is said to cheat.
laws.keywords: cheating, fraud, deception, inducement, fraudulent, dishonest, 420
laws.punishment: Imprisonment up to three years, or fine, or both.

laws.title: Forgery
laws.description: Whoever makes any false document or false electronic record or part of a document or electronic record, with intent to cause damage or injury, to the public or to any person, or to support any claim or title, or to cause any person to part with property, or to enter into any express or implied contract, or with intent to commit fraud or that fraud may be committed, commits forgery.
laws.keywords: forgery, false document, fake document, electronic record, fraud
laws.punishment: Imprisonment up to two years, or fine, or both.

laws.title: Forgery of valuable security, will etc.
laws.description: Whoever forges a document which purports to be a valuable security or a will, or an authority to adopt a son, or which purports to give authority to any person to make or transfer any valuable security, or to receive the principal, interest or dividends thereon, or to receive or deliver any money, movable property, or valuable security, or any document purporting to be an acquittance or receipt acknowledging the payment of money.
laws.keywords: forgery valuable security, forged will, fake will, forged power of attorney
laws.punishment: Imprisonment for life, or imprisonment up to ten years, and shall also be liable to fine.

laws.title: Criminal intimidation
laws.description: Whoever threatens another with any injury to his person, reputation or property, or to the person or reputation of any one in whom that person is interested, with intent to cause alarm to that person, or to cause that person to do any act which he is not legally bound to do, or to omit to do any act which that person is legally entitled to do, as the means of avoiding the execution of such threat, commits criminal intimidation.
laws.keywords: criminal intimidation, threat, alarm, coercion, intimidation, threatening
laws.punishment: Imprisonment up to two years, or fine, or both.

laws.title: Intentional insult with intent to provoke breach of peace
laws.description: Whoever intentionally insults, and thereby gives provocation to any person, intending or knowing it to be likely that such provocation will cause him to break the public peace, or to commit any other offence.
laws.keywords: insult, provocation, breach of peace, intentional insult
laws.punishment: Imprisonment up to two years, or fine, or both.

laws.title: Defamation
laws.description: Whoever, by words either spoken or intended to be read, or by signs or by visible representations, makes or publishes any imputation concerning any person intending to harm, or knowing or having reason to believe that such imputation will harm, the reputation of such person, is said to defame that person.
laws.keywords: defamation, libel, slander, reputation, imputation, harm to reputation
laws.punishment: Simple imprisonment up to two years, or fine, or both.

laws.title: Assault or criminal force to woman with intent to outrage modesty
laws.description: Whoever assaults or uses criminal force to any woman, intending to outrage or knowing it to be likely that he will thereby outrage her modesty.
laws.keywords: sexual assault, outrage modesty, molestation, criminal force on woman, eve-teasing
laws.punishment: Imprisonment of either description for a term not less than one year but which may extend to five years, and shall also be liable to fine.

laws.title: Word, gesture or act intended to insult modesty of woman
laws.description: Whoever, intending to insult the modesty of any woman, utters any words, makes any sound or gesture, or exhibits any object, intending that such word or sound shall be heard, or that such gesture or object shall be seen, by such woman, or intrudes upon the privacy of such woman.
laws.keywords: insult modesty, verbal harassment, obscene words, gesture, eve-teasing, harassment of women
laws.punishment: Simple imprisonment up to three years, and fine.

laws.title: Voluntarily causing hurt
laws.description: Whoever does any act with the intention of thereby causing hurt to any person, or with the knowledge that he is likely thereby to cause hurt to any person, and does thereby cause hurt to any person, is said to voluntarily cause hurt.
laws.keywords: hurt, causing hurt, bodily harm, assault, injury, physical violence
laws.punishment: Imprisonment up to one year, or fine up to ten thousand rupees, or both.

laws.title: Voluntarily causing grievous hurt
laws.description: Whoever voluntarily causes hurt, if the hurt which he intends to cause or knows himself to be likely to cause is grievous hurt, and if the hurt which he causes is grievous hurt, is said to voluntarily cause grievous hurt.
laws.keywords: grievous hurt, serious injury, permanent damage, dangerous injury
laws.punishment: Imprisonment up to seven years, and shall also be liable to fine.

laws.title: Act endangering life or personal safety of others
laws.description: Whoever does any act so rashly or negligently as to endanger human life or the personal safety of others.
laws.keywords: rash act, negligence, endangering life, reckless, personal safety
laws.punishment: Imprisonment up to three months, or fine up to two thousand five hundred rupees, or both.

laws.title: Husband or relative of husband subjecting woman to cruelty
laws.description: Whoever, being the husband or the relative of the husband of a woman, subjects such woman to cruelty shall be punished. Cruelty means any wilful conduct which is of such a nature as is likely to drive the woman to commit suicide or to cause grave injury or danger to life, limb or health (whether mental or physical) of the woman, or harassment of the woman with a view to coercing her to meet any unlawful demand for property or valuable security or on account of failure by her or any person related to her to meet such demand.
laws.keywords: cruelty, domestic violence, harassment for dowry, mental cruelty, physical cruelty, 498A
laws.punishment: Imprisonment up to three years, and shall also be liable to fine.

laws.title: Conditions for a Hindu marriage
laws.description: A marriage may be solemnized between any two Hindus, if the following conditions are fulfilled: (i) neither party has a spouse living at the time of the marriage; (ii) at the time of the marriage, neither party is incapable of giving a valid consent due to unsoundness of mind, or suffering from mental disorder, or recurrent attacks of insanity or epilepsy; (iii) the bridegroom has completed the age of twenty-one years and the bride the age of eighteen years at the time of the marriage; (iv) the parties are not within the degrees of prohibited relationship unless permitted by custom; (v) the parties are not sapindas of each other, unless permitted by custom.
laws.keywords: hindu marriage conditions, valid marriage, marriage requirements, consent, age of marriage
laws.punishment: Marriage voidable or void if conditions not met

laws.title: Ceremonies for a Hindu marriage
laws.description: A Hindu marriage may be solemnized in accordance with the customary rites and ceremonies of either party thereto. Where such rites and ceremonies include the Saptapadi (taking seven steps by the bridegroom and the bride jointly before the sacred fire), the marriage becomes complete and binding when the seventh step is taken.
laws.keywords: marriage ceremony, saptapadi, customary rites, sacred fire, hindu rituals
laws.punishment: N/A - Procedural

laws.title: Restitution of conjugal rights
laws.description: When either the husband or the wife has, without reasonable excuse, withdrawn from the society of the other, the aggrieved party may apply, by petition to the district court, for restitution of conjugal rights and the court, on being satisfied of the truth of the statements made in such petition and that there is no legal ground why the application should not be granted, may decree restitution of conjugal rights accordingly.
laws.keywords: restitution of conjugal rights, desertion, separation, living apart, matrimonial remedy
laws.punishment: Decree for restitution; disobedience may lead to divorce

laws.title: Judicial separation
laws.description: Either party to a marriage may present a petition praying for a decree for judicial separation on any of the grounds specified in sub-section (1) of section 13, and the court shall, if satisfied that any such ground is made out, grant a decree for judicial separation.
laws.keywords: judicial separation, legal separation, separate living, matrimonial relief
laws.punishment: Decree for judicial separation - parties live separately

laws.title: Divorce
laws.description: Any marriage solemnized may, on a petition presented by either the husband or the wife, be dissolved by a decree of divorce on the grounds that: (i) the other party has committed adultery; (ii) has treated the petitioner with cruelty; (iii) has deserted the petitioner for a continuous period of not less than two years; (iv) has ceased to be a Hindu by conversion to another religion; (v) has been incurably of unsound mind or suffering continuously from mental disorder; (vi) has been suffering from a virulent and incurable form of leprosy; (vii) has been suffering from venereal disease in a communicable form; (viii) has renounced the world by entering any religious order; (ix) has not been heard of as being alive for a period of seven years or more.
laws.keywords: divorce, dissolution of marriage, grounds for divorce, adultery, cruelty, desertion
laws.punishment: Decree of divorce - marriage dissolved

laws.title: Divorce by mutual consent
laws.description: Subject to the provisions of this Act a petition for dissolution of marriage by a decree of divorce may be presented to the district court by both the parties to a marriage together, whether such marriage was solemnized before or after the commencement of the Marriage Laws (Amendment) Act, 1976, on the ground that they have been living separately for a period of one year or more, that they have not been able to live together and that they have mutually agreed that the marriage should be dissolved.
laws.keywords: mutual consent divorce, amicable divorce, joint petition, separation for one year
laws.punishment: Decree of divorce after cooling-off period (6 months to 18 months)

laws.title: Maintenance pendente lite and expenses of proceedings
laws.description: Where in any proceeding under this Act it appears to the court that either the wife or the husband, as the case may be, has no independent income sufficient for her or his support and the necessary expenses of the proceeding, it may, on the application of the wife or the husband, order the respondent to pay to the petitioner the expenses of the proceeding, and monthly during the proceeding such sum as, having regard to the petitioners own income and the income of the respondent, it may seem to the court to be reasonable.
laws.keywords: interim maintenance, maintenance pendente lite, litigation expenses, support during divorce
laws.punishment: Order for interim maintenance/expenses

laws.title: Permanent alimony and maintenance
