laws.description: Any court exercising jurisdiction under this Act may, at the time of passing any decree or at any time subsequent thereto, on application made to it for the purpose by either the wife or the husband, order that the respondent shall pay to the applicant for her or his maintenance and support such gross sum or such monthly or periodical sum for a term not exceeding the life of the applicant as, having regard to the respondents own income and other property, the income and other property of the applicant, the conduct of the parties and other circumstances of the case, it may seem to the court to be just.
laws.keywords: permanent alimony, permanent maintenance, spousal support, post-divorce maintenance
laws.punishment: Order for permanent alimony/maintenance

laws.title: Custody of children
laws.description: In any proceeding under this Act, the court may, from time to time, pass such interim orders and make such provisions in the decree as it may deem just and proper with respect to the custody, maintenance and education of minor children, consistently with their wishes, wherever possible, and may, after the decree, upon application by petition for the purpose, make from time to time, all such orders and provisions with respect to the custody, maintenance and education of such children as might have been made by such decree or interim orders in case the proceeding for obtaining such decree were still pending.
laws.keywords: child custody, minor children, custody order, child maintenance, education, welfare of child
laws.punishment: Custody order based on welfare of child

laws.title: Responsibility for payment of wages
laws.description: Every employer shall be responsible for the payment to persons employed by him of all wages required to be paid under this Act. In the case of persons employed in factories, industrial or other establishments, the manager designated as responsible shall be liable.
laws.keywords: wage payment, employer responsibility, salary, payment obligation, factory wages
laws.punishment: Penalty for delay: fine; civil liability for unpaid wages

laws.title: Time of payment of wages
laws.description: The wages of every person employed upon or in any railway, factory or industrial or other establishment shall be paid before the expiry of the seventh day, or tenth day in establishments employing more than 1000 persons, after the last day of the wage period in respect of which the wages are payable.
laws.keywords: wage timing, salary date, payment deadline, monthly salary, wage period
laws.punishment: Penalty for delayed payment

laws.title: Deductions which may be made from wages
laws.description: The wages of an employed person shall be paid to him without deductions of any kind except those authorized by or under this Act. The following deductions only are authorized: (a) fines; (b) deductions for absence from duty; (c) deductions for damage to or loss of goods; (d) deductions for house-accommodation; (e) deductions for amenities and services; (f) deductions for recovery of advances; (g) deductions for income-tax; (h) deductions for provident fund contributions; (i) deductions made with written authorization for payment to cooperative societies or insurance schemes.
laws.keywords: wage deduction, salary cut, authorized deductions, illegal deduction, PF deduction
laws.punishment: Penalty for unauthorized deductions

laws.title: Claims arising out of deductions or delay in payment
laws.description: The appropriate Government may, by notification, appoint a Commissioner or Additional Labour Commissioner to be the Authority to hear and decide all claims arising out of deductions from wages or delay in payment of wages of persons employed.
laws.keywords: wage claim, labour commissioner, wage dispute, deduction claim, delayed salary claim
laws.punishment: Authority may order payment with compensation

laws.title: Cases in which specific performance enforceable
laws.description: The specific performance of a contract shall be enforced by the court subject to the provisions contained in sub-section (2) of section 11, section 14 and section 16.
laws.keywords: specific performance, contract enforcement, court order, performance of contract
laws.punishment: Court may order specific performance of contract

laws.title: Contracts not specifically enforceable
laws.description: The following contracts cannot be specifically enforced: (a) where monetary compensation is adequate relief; (b) a contract which involves performance of continuous duties which the court cannot supervise; (c) a contract so dependent on personal qualifications of the parties that the court cannot enforce specific performance.
laws.keywords: non-enforceable contract, personal service contract, continuous duties, damages adequate
laws.punishment: Specific performance denied; damages may be awarded instead

laws.title: Preventive relief - how granted
laws.description: Preventive relief is granted at the discretion of the court by injunction, temporary or perpetual.
laws.keywords: injunction, preventive relief, temporary injunction, permanent injunction, restraining order
laws.punishment: Court may grant temporary or perpetual injunction

laws.title: Perpetual injunction when granted
laws.description: A perpetual injunction may be granted to prevent the breach of an obligation existing in favour of the applicant, whether expressly or by implication.
laws.keywords: perpetual injunction, permanent injunction, breach prevention, obligation enforcement
laws.punishment: Court may grant perpetual injunction
